title: Error: when using volume from host folder, some commands panic (macos)
body:
### Issue Description

I'm using a terraform container to test with "terraform plan" in a repository I've mounted as a volume on the container.

```bash
podman run -d -v MY_FOLDER:/cloud-lab-vpc-terraform myimage:latest
```

If I exec into the container and run "terraform plan" directly positioned on the folder I've mounted terraform crashes:

```bash
/cloud-lab-vpc-terraform # terraform plan
Acquiring state lock. This may take a few moments...
Releasing state lock. This may take a few moments...
╷
│ Error: Failed to load plugin schemas
│ 
│ Error while loading schemas for plugin components: 4 problems:
│ 
│ - Failed to obtain provider schema: Could not load the schema for provider registry.terraform.io/e-breuninger/netbox: failed to instantiate provider "registry.terraform.io/e-breuninger/netbox" to obtain schema: Unrecognized remote plugin message: 
│ 
│ This usually means that the plugin is either invalid or simply
│ needs to be recompiled to support the latest protocol..
│ - Failed to obtain provider schema: Could not load the schema for provider registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/aws: failed to instantiate provider "registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/aws" to obtain schema: Unrecognized remote plugin message: 
│ 
│ This usually means that the plugin is either invalid or simply
│ needs to be recompiled to support the latest protocol..
│ - Failed to obtain provider schema: Could not load the schema for provider registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/google: failed to instantiate provider "registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/google" to obtain schema: Unrecognized remote plugin message: 
│ 
│ This usually means that the plugin is either invalid or simply
│ needs to be recompiled to support the latest protocol..
│ - Failed to obtain provider schema: Could not load the schema for provider registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/vsphere: failed to instantiate provider "registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/vsphere" to obtain schema: Unrecognized remote plugin message: 
│ 
│ This usually means that the plugin is either invalid or simply
│ needs to be recompiled to support the latest protocol...
╵

Stack trace from the terraform-provider-netbox_v2.0.7 plugin:

fatal error: invalid pointer found on stack

runtime stack:
runtime.throw({0x174ec33?, 0x24e31c0?})
	runtime/panic.go:992 +0x71 fp=0xc0000835f0 sp=0xc0000835c0 pc=0x434111
runtime.adjustpointers(0xc0000839e0?, 0x9f75c?, 0x1e8b03a?, {0x20fbd40?, 0x24e31c0?})
	runtime/stack.go:628 +0x1d0 fp=0xc000083658 sp=0xc0000835f0 pc=0x44c2f0
runtime.adjustframe(0xc0000839e0, 0xc000083ad0)
	runtime/stack.go:670 +0xc9 fp=0xc0000836e8 sp=0xc000083658 pc=0x44c409
runtime.gentraceback(0x0?, 0x0?, 0x0?, 0x0?, 0x0, 0x0, 0x7fffffff, 0x17ba950, 0x0?, 0x0)
	runtime/traceback.go:334 +0xc47 fp=0xc000083a48 sp=0xc0000836e8 pc=0x456ec7
runtime.copystack(0xc000002340, 0x0?)
	runtime/stack.go:930 +0x2f5 fp=0xc000083c00 sp=0xc000083a48 pc=0x44cc15
runtime.shrinkstack(0xc000002340)
	runtime/stack.go:1212 +0x126 fp=0xc000083c20 sp=0xc000083c00 pc=0x44dae6
runtime.scanstack(0xc000002340, 0xc000061238)
	runtime/mgcmark.go:754 +0xc7 fp=0xc000083e28 sp=0xc000083c20 pc=0x41df27
runtime.markroot.func1()
	runtime/mgcmark.go:241 +0xc5 fp=0xc000083e78 sp=0xc000083e28 pc=0x41cf25
runtime.markroot(0xc000061238, 0x14, 0x1)
	runtime/mgcmark.go:214 +0x1a5 fp=0xc000083f18 sp=0xc000083e78 pc=0x41cba5
runtime.gcDrain(0xc000061238, 0x3)
	runtime/mgcmark.go:1047 +0x39f fp=0xc000083f78 sp=0xc000083f18 pc=0x41ebbf
runtime.gcBgMarkWorker.func2()
	runtime/mgc.go:1276 +0xa5 fp=0xc000083fc8 sp=0xc000083f78 pc=0x41ba45
runtime.systemstack()
	runtime/asm_amd64.s:469 +0x49 fp=0xc000083fd0 sp=0xc000083fc8 pc=0x461ae9

```

If I copy the contents of that folder in a new one (inside the container) and run "terraform plan" again, it works.

```bash
/ # cp -r /cloud-lab-vpc-terraform /test_tf && cd /test_tf
/test_tf # terraform plan
Acquiring state lock. This may take a few moments...
google_compute_address.name: Refreshing state... [id=projects/name/regions/region/addresses/resource]
```

I'm opening this issue here because I'm thinking that the problem cloud be on how podman mounts the folder into the container. If I do the same thing with docker it works fine, without the need of copying the folder inside the container.
Also tried with a different of terraform projects, terraform bin versions, image builds based on different base OS, and always the same error and workaround.

Regards,


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. create a container and mount a volume from a local folder
2. exec into the container and workdir
3. run terraform plan


### Describe the results you received

Terraform crashes

### Describe the results you expected

Should run without issues

### podman info output

```yaml
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.4.4
API Version:  4.4.4
Go Version:   go1.19
Git Commit:   c8223435f49a860c8ac4281a85db39232745a7cb
Built:        Mon Mar 27 20:44:30 2023
OS/Arch:      darwin/amd64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.4.2
API Version:  4.4.2
Go Version:   go1.19.6
Built:        Wed Mar  1 08:22:59 2023
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

MacOS 13.3.1 on MacBook Pro intel i7 32GB RAM

### Additional information

This issue always happens.

comments.body:
I don't think there is anything we can do about it. The panic seems like a bug with your application, it should handle errors correctly.

We just mount the host volume as 9p file system. Maybe this caused due the slow speed? (https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/16994)

The reason I think is something related to podman and how mounts folders, is that I had no problem in doing the same with docker. And also terraform isn't the only binary, if I exec a git status in that mount from inside the container it has to rebuild the indexes, something that didn't happen with docker.
I didn't try running the VM in rootful mode to see if that fixes it.
It could be an issue with Plan 9 File system versus Docker's use of virtiofsd?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
podman 5.0 uses virtiofs and the apple hypervisor so it should be much faster
